---
title: "Circle Calculus Living Book"
---

Circle Calculus is a proof-linked, executable, interactive living book for finite circular address spaces, coils, winding, and the dimension-indexed sphere ladder.

This site is meant to be read like a textbook. Start with the smallest object, learn one operation at a time, use the diagrams to build intuition, and then open theorem cards when you want to see exactly what has been checked.

The site is the public explanation layer generated from repository artifacts. It does not replace the formal project, and it must not promote a theorem beyond the status recorded in the generated theorem manifest.

```text
Lean proofs -> theorem manifests -> dictionary -> Python models -> papers -> interactive site
```

::: {.book-preface}

## How To Read This Book

Read the lessons in order the first time through. Each lesson has four roles:

- **Picture:** a diagram or widget that lets you change small examples.
- **Idea:** the plain-language explanation of what the picture is showing.
- **Checkpoint:** a small task that tests whether the concept is usable.
- **Source trail:** theorem cards, dictionary entries, Python examples, papers, and Lean links for audit.

The indexes are reference shelves. They are useful after you have learned the idea, but the main path is the chapter sequence.

:::

## Learning Ladder

| Part | What You Learn | First Page |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 0. Reading proof-linked math | What counts as intuition, code, paper prose, or proof. | [Reader Path](reader_path.qmd) |
| 1. Finite circular worlds | A circle as `n` reusable addresses, not a Euclidean curve. | [Finite Circles](chapters/S1/01_finite_circles.qmd) |
| 2. Motion | Rotation as addition modulo `n`. | [Rotation As Addition](chapters/S1/02_rotation_as_addition.qmd) |
| 3. Repetition | Coils, orbits, closure, and period. | [Coils And Closure](chapters/S1/03_coils_orbits_closure.qmd) |
| 4. Structure | GCD, cycle decomposition, and prime full coils. | [Period And Primes](chapters/S1/04_period_gcd_prime_full_coils.qmd) |
| 5. Lifts | Residue plus winding, so repeated circular motion remembers turns. | [Winding Lift](chapters/S1/05_winding_lift.qmd) |
| 6. Higher dimensions | Suspensions, spheres, quaternions, Hopf coordinates, and warning boundaries. | [Roadmap](roadmap.qmd) |
| 7. Applications | Data, compute, AI, and rendering tracks guarded by proof and benchmark status. | [Applications Overview](chapters/applications/index.qmd) |

## What You Can Trust Here

- Theorem cards and status badges come from generated theorem data.
- Dictionary boxes come from shared dictionary YAML, with generated backlinks into theorem, paper, widget, and glyph usage.
- Python examples and browser widgets are executable explanations, not formal proofs.
- Source links point back to the public repository so every serious claim can be followed into Lean, paper prose, manifests, or reference code.

## Reading Modes

**Intuition Mode**

Use the widgets and diagrams to build a geometric picture. These are explanations, not proofs.

**Formal Mode**

Open theorem cards, Lean declaration names, manifests, and paper links. A theorem is treated as proved only when the manifest and Lean build say so.

**Code Mode**

Compare widget behavior against the Python reference models and tests. Python examples are executable support, not formal proof.

## Start

- Begin with the [guided reader path](reader_path.qmd).
- Then start [S1 finite circles](chapters/S1/index.qmd).
- Use [Verify A Claim](verify_claim.qmd) when you want to audit a theorem, paper paragraph, widget, or diagram.
- Browse the [dictionary index](dictionary.qmd) when a term matters.
- Browse the [theorem index](theorems.qmd) when a theorem id matters.
- Inspect the [project roadmap](roadmap.qmd).
- Read the proof-status policy in [Living Book Policy](https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/docs/LIVING_BOOK_POLICY.md).

<script type="module" src="widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
